items.title: Senators Are Talking But No Shutdown Deal Sighted | Balance of Power 01/28/26
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>"Balance of Power: Late Edition" focuses on the intersection of politics and global business. On today's show, Tiffany Wilding, PIMCO Managing Dir. & Economist, on the Federal Reserve pausing rates. Sen. Gary Peters (D-MI) on potential partial government shutdown latest & Alex Pretti investigation, and Rep. Mike Lawler (R-NY) on government shutdown possibility and NYC Mayor Mamdani pushing tax hikes on wealthy New Yorkers.

Chapters:
00:00:00 - Balance of Power: Late Edition Begins
00:01:56 - Ed Ludlow on Tesla to Invest $2B in Musk’s XAI, Microsoft Shares Sink After Reporting 2Q Earnings
00:05:42 - Michael McKee on Fed Votes 10-2 to Hold Benchmark Rate, Pres. Trump on Hassett, Who Could Be Next Fed Chair
00:10:04 - Power Moves: Top Financial Stories of the Day
00:12:22 - Kate Sullivan on Trump Warns Minneapolis Mayor is ‘Playing With Fire,’ Trump Talks ‘Trump Accounts’ for Kids
00:17:01 - Tiffany Wilding, PIMCO Managing Dir. & Economist, on Reaction to Fed Decision, Who Could Be Next Fed Chair
00:25:09 - Sen. Gary Peters (D-MI) Talks Latest on Potential Govt. Shutdown, Wanting DHS Reforms, Alex Pretti Investigation
00:35:25 - Wendy Sherman, Harvard Belfer Center Senior Fellow, on Reaction to Rubio Testifying Before Senate Committee
00:41:32 - Rep. Mike Lawler (R-NY) on If The Government Should Shutdown, NYC Mayor Mamdani Proposes Tax Hike on Wealthy NYers
--------
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

items.title: Fed Holds Rates as Big Tech Earnings Hit Markets | The Close 1/28/2026
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are DoubleLine Capital’s Ken Shinoda, Gabelli Funds’ John Belton, DA Davidson’s Gil Luria, Ed Ludlow, Gerber Kawasaki Wealth and Investment Management’s Ross Gerber,The Futurum Group’s Daniel Newman, Former Federal Reserve Vice Chair Lael Brainard.

items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow reports on Tesla's plans to invest about $2 billion into xAI, giving Elon Musk’s artificial-intelligence startup a cash infusion despite a shareholder vote last year that failed to win approval.

items.title: Bloomberg Surveillance: The Fed Decides 1/28/2025
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Jon Ferro and Tom Keene host a special edition of "Bloomberg Surveillance." Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said labor market weakness or a decline in tariff-induced inflation could lead to looser monetary policy after the central bank left rates unchanged. He said the central bank would consider interest-rate reductions if the labor market weakened or if the inflationary effect on goods from tariffs began to wane.

Chapters:
00:00:01 - Bloomberg Surveillance: The Fed Decides
00:00:15 - Tom Keene, Bloomberg News

items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:

items.title: LIVE: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady | Special Coverage
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Federal Reserve officials left interest rates unchanged and pointed to improvements in the US economy as they signaled a more cautious approach to potential future adjustments.

Bloomberg TV analyzes the latest decision followed by Chair Jerome Powell's press conference in Washington.</article>

items.title: Trump’s Border Patrol Commander Bovino Is Leaving Minnesota After Backlash
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Bill Faries, Bloomberg News Senior Editor, discusses the growing backlash to the latest Minneapolis ICE shooting. 

Greg Bovino, the US Border Patrol commander who became the face of President Donald Trump’s sweeping immigration crackdown in Minneapolis, is leaving the city following a mounting public outcry over the killing of two US citizens by federal agents in recent weeks.
--------

items.title: Starbucks’ Big Sales Beat Stokes Confidence in Turnaround
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Starbucks Corp. Chief Executive Officer Brian Niccol delivered the best evidence yet that his turnaround plan is taking hold, with the coffee chain posting unexpectedly strong growth and a solid outlook for the rest of the year. Bloomberg's Michael Halen joins Bloomberg Intelligence to discuss.
--------

items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell says that the committee was broadly in favor of holding rates steady this time around bloom.bg/3ZEDxg6</article>

items.title: Waabi CEO: “It’s a miss if you don't invest in Canada”
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>Waabi CEO Raquel Urtasun tells tech investors that "it’s a miss if you don't invest in Canada." Watch our full interview here: https://bloom.bg/4qU6HnK</article>

items.title: LIVE: California Governor Gavin Newsom | 2:15 pm ET, Jan. 29
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>California Governor Gavin Newsom joins Bloomberg Businessweek Editor Brad Stone for a wide ranging conversation from our San Francisco bureau.</article>

items.title: Trump Warns Iran That Time Is Running Out
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Bloomberg's Nick Wadhams says that the US is still in 'wait and see mode' on the potential for a strike against Iran as President Trump warns Iran that a failure to restart nuclear talks could end in direct action. Wadhams says that its hard to rule anything out with this administration, since attacks on both Iran and Venezuela have 'rewrote the rules of the game' when it comes to foreign policy. 
--------
